title: Always use HTTPS for external service calls
rule: NEVER make production API calls over plain http://. Always use https:// to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks and credential leakage.

title: Never use eval() or exec() for dynamic code
rule: NEVER use eval() or exec() to execute dynamic strings as code — it enables remote code execution. Use JSON.parse() for data, fixed subprocess argument lists for shell commands.

title: Never use Math.random() for security values
rule: NEVER use Math.random() for security tokens, session IDs, nonces, or keys — it is not cryptographically secure. Use crypto.randomBytes() or crypto.randomUUID() in Node.js; secrets.token_hex() in Python.
